message:
feat(connection): fast-path gate bitfield scaffolding (IMPL-28 / AG-O3)

Adds an `AtomicU32 fast_path_gate` to `Connection`, plus a
`fast_path_gate` module of bit constants describing each precondition
for the general fast lane (`IN_TRANSACTION`, `NO_STATEMENT_SAVEPOINT`,
`TRACING_DISABLED`, `SCHEMA_STABLE`) and an `ALL_GATES_OPEN` aggregate.

Motivation: the per-statement dispatch hot path currently walks the
dozen-plus independent boolean guards (tracing subscribers present?
schema stable? explicit txn active? etc.) on every execution. The gate
collapses that AND-tree into a single `Relaxed` load against
`ALL_GATES_OPEN`, so the innermost check can be one atomic read instead
of N field loads.

This commit is ADDITIVE scaffolding only — the field is maintained but
not yet consumed at any dispatch site (annotated with `#[allow(dead_code)]`
pointing at the follow-up migration commits). Follow-ups will migrate
each gate-site one at a time so semantic equivalence can be proven
site-by-site rather than in one wholesale swap.

Safety: bits default CLOSED (0). A closed bit always routes to the
slow/instrumented path, which is safe by construction; bits are only
set when the corresponding state-transition site has re-established the
invariant (e.g. the transaction-begin path opens `IN_TRANSACTION`).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
